title: history: rewrite mappings
body:
Rewrite the backend for displaying the history of an image to simplify
the code and be closer to docker's behaviour.  Instead of driving
index-based heuristics, create a reverse mapping from top-layers to the
corresponding image IDs and lookup the layers on-demand.  Also use the
uncompressed layer size to be closer to Docker's behaviour.

Fixes: #3359
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
First impression, quick test, looks great! Thank you!

Real problem, can reproduce:
```
$ PM=./bin/podman-remote
$ $PM run -d alpine_labels top;$PM run alpine_labels date;$PM container prune;echo;$PM rm -fa
b349775bb51bc689f460a64c1cbb54b7eb8a30da959a39567efd4956f1df4de5
Wed Nov 13 13:06:57 UTC 2019

49d99fd914755aebf625ca8a397fa527b02bd74c89edd20bbbdfad0d1c3a5628
b349775bb51bc689f460a64c1cbb54b7eb8a30da959a39567efd4956f1df4de5
```
It's a race: `container prune`, when run too soon after `podman run`, does not prune anything; so it's the `rm` that cleans up both containers. It only seems to happen with podman-remote, never with podman. I do not see how this could possibly be related to your PR.
